author: y4rr
content:
Spun out of #590. That issue landed the simpler ops in \`ferrotorch-vision/src/ops.rs\`: \`box_convert\`, \`box_iou\`, \`box_area\`, \`clip_boxes_to_image\`, \`remove_small_boxes\`, \`nms\`, \`batched_nms\`, \`sigmoid_focal_loss\`, \`focal_loss\`. The remaining ops need either bilinear sampling kernels (RoI pooling) or extra IoU geometry.

## Remaining

### \`roi_align(input, boxes, output_size, spatial_scale, sampling_ratio, aligned)\`
Take a feature map \`[N, C, H, W]\` and a list of boxes (typically \`[K, 5]\` with \`[batch_idx, x1, y1, x2, y2]\`), produce \`[K, C, output_h, output_w]\` by bilinearly sampling each box. Used by Faster R-CNN / Mask R-CNN heads.

Implementation outline:
1. For each output bin \`(oh, ow)\` of each box, compute the corresponding sampling grid in the input feature map.
2. Bilinearly interpolate at each sample.
3. Average the samples per bin to get the output.

### \`roi_pool(input, boxes, output_size, spatial_scale)\`
Older variant: pool max within each bin instead of bilinear sampling. Cheaper but less accurate.

### \`generalized_box_iou\`
GIoU = IoU - (area_enclosing - area_union) / area_enclosing. Used as a regression loss in DETR / DINO.

### \`distance_box_iou\` / \`complete_box_iou\`
DIoU and CIoU variants — incorporate center-distance + aspect-ratio penalty terms.

## Notes
- \`roi_align\` and \`roi_pool\` benefit greatly from a GPU kernel (CUDA + cubecl). The CPU reference impl can land first; GPU version follows.
- \`generalized_box_iou\` and friends are pure tensor math, composable from existing differentiable ops, so they can be added as forward-only first then wrapped in autograd later.
